name: Indian IT Sector (TCS/Infosys/Wipro)
summary: Day 68 Wednesday. **INDIAN IT LAGGING DESPITE BROADER AI-CYCLE TAILWIND — APPLE-SAMSUNG-INTEL CHIP-PRODUCTION TALKS REDIRECTED AI FLOWS TO MANUFACTURERS, NOT IT SERVICES.** TCS, Infosys, Wipro under pressure today even as KOSPI broke 7,000 with Samsung $1T market cap and SMH +3.1%. The structural read: AI capex flows reward DIRECT exposure (semiconductor manufacturers in Korea/Taiwan/US, hyperscaler infrastructure in US) over INDIRECT exposure (IT services implementation lag). Indian IT has the 2026 capex → 2027 services-growth lag, but the real-time market is pricing the direct-exposure beneficiaries. This is the cleanest signal that the AI-infrastructure-build narrative does NOT automatically lift Indian IT services in this cycle. FOMC backdrop: rate-cut catalyst pushed deeper into 2026 (CME FedWatch ~28% June cut); US-recession-risk on discretionary IT spending intact. Q4 FY26 earnings weakness (Infosys, HDFC, Tech Mahindra, Wipro, HCL Tech) remains the dominant DOMESTIC driver. TCS Q4 +25% operating margin + $2.3B annualized AI revenues showed AI materiality threshold crossed at largest Indian IT vendor. Indian IT remains margin-pressure trade with AI-implementation upside on 2027 horizon — but for now, the direct-vs-indirect bifurcation is the dominant flow.
